name: Fix drifted retry-filing cap — filing.ts hardcodes 3 instead of FILING_CONFIG.maxRetries
description: filing.ts:94 hardcodes 3; sessions.ts:380 reads FILING_CONFIG.maxRetries (config/filing.ts:3 = 3). Values agree TODAY but filing.ts has no config import, so the cap can silently diverge on any future config change — latent drift bug. Approved cut (minimum fix): import FILING_CONFIG in filing.ts, replace the literal. Do NOT move cap logic into the data-access layer (separate retry-logic work). filing.ts coincides with IF security work (F-126) — coordinate PR timing.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: apps/api/src/routes/filing.ts:94 hardcodes the literal 3 for the retry cap and imports nothing from ../config/filing, while sessions.ts:380 reads FILING_CONFIG.maxRetries (config/filing.ts:3 = 3); the two agree today but filing.ts cannot follow a future config change — latent drift. Behavioral variants: SINGLE variant — one hardcoded literal at filing.ts:94; no other divergent retry-cap flow exists (sessions.ts already config-sourced). Fix (operator-approved minimum): import FILING_CONFIG in filing.ts and replace the :94 literal so both filing.ts and sessions.ts derive the cap from the single config constant. Regression test: asserts the cap derives from config — changing FILING_CONFIG.maxRetries changes both paths (test fails against the hardcoded literal, passes after the fix). Do NOT move cap logic into the data-access layer (separate retry-logic work). api tests green.
